name: Write-skeleton-first for research agents
description: When dispatching research agents, mandate writing a skeleton file with frontmatter + section headers BEFORE any research. Fill sections incrementally. Agents told 'write as you go' (advisory) had 62.5% failure rate. Agents told 'WRITE FIRST before reading or searching' (imperative) had 100% success rate.

name: research-doc-skeleton-first
description: Write comprehensive research docs with frontmatter, executive summary, detailed ToC with internal links, code examples, comparison matrices, ASCII diagrams, and sources. Use skeleton-first: write headers and ToC before content.

name: write-skeleton-first
description: For research agents: Create frontmatter + section headers before filling content prevents scope creep and maintains focus. Create all skeletons first (2 docs + index), then fill systematically.

name: chimera-agent-wrapper-pattern
description: ChimeraAgent wraps Strands concepts with multi-tenant context (tenantId always required, sessionId generated if not provided). SystemPromptTemplate uses {{variable}} syntax for tenant/session injection. AgentCore Memory namespace follows convention: tenant-{tenantId}-user-{userId}.

name: python-agentcore-entrypoint
description: Python agents for AgentCore Runtime use @entrypoint decorator on async generator function. Function receives context object with context.auth.claims (JWT), context.input_text (user message), context.session (metadata). Must yield response chunks for streaming. Container runs via 'python -m bedrock_agentcore.runtime' CMD.

name: strands-agentcore-integration
description: Strands Agent integrates with AgentCore via AgentCoreMemorySessionManager. Pass memory_id, namespace (tenant-{id}-user-{id}), strategies list, conversation_window_size, retention_policy as params. Agent created with BedrockModel, system_prompt, tools list, session_manager, max_iterations=20. Use agent.stream() for async iteration.

name: multi-tenant-agent-context
description: Extract tenantId, tier, userId from context.auth.claims (Cognito JWT). Load tenant config from DynamoDB (TENANT#id/PROFILE). Select model based on tier (basic=nova-lite, advanced=sonnet, premium=opus). Configure memory namespace, strategies, and window per tier. Build system prompt with tenant context and budget.

name: python-terminal-chat-repl
description: Terminal chat REPL for local Python agent testing requires mock AgentCore context objects (MockContext with auth.claims, input_text, session). Streams responses via async for loop over @entrypoint handler.

name: strands-tool-auto-discovery
description: AWS SDK tools use Strands @tool decorator for automatic registration. Import tool functions, add to tools list in load_tenant_tools(). Strands extracts function signature and docstring automatically.

name: python-codecommit-codepipeline-tools
description: Python agent self-evolution tools use Strands @tool decorator with boto3 clients for git operations (git_clone_repo, git_commit_push via subprocess) and CI/CD (trigger_pipeline, check_pipeline_status). Each tool returns formatted strings, includes try-except error handling, and respects IAM policies at tenant level.

name: python-agent-identity-injection
description: Load SOUL.md + AGENTS.md at runtime in build_system_prompt(). Use pathlib.Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent to navigate from packages/agents/ to project root. Graceful fallback for missing files prevents deployment failures.

name: python-background-task-eventbridge
description: Python agents delegate long-running work via EventBridge + Step Functions. Pattern: (1) Store task metadata in sessions table (PK=TENANT#{id}, SK=BGTASK#{taskId}), (2) Publish EventBridge event with DetailType='Background Task Started', (3) Event triggers appropriate Step Functions state machine (pipeline_build, data_analysis), (4) Agent polls task status via DDB queries. Tools return immediately with task_id, enabling non-blocking async execution.

name: python-aws-data-tools-pattern
description: Python Strands AWS tools (Athena/Glue/Redshift) use simple boto3 clients without tenantId/agentId params (tenant context injected at AgentCore runtime level). Tools return human-readable formatted strings for agent consumption (not JSON), following Strands philosophy of conversational tool responses.

name: python-lambda-stepfunctions-codebuild-tools
description: Python Strands tools for Lambda (7 ops), Step Functions (9 ops), and CodeBuild (6 ops) follow established pattern with @tool decorator, boto3 clients, and formatted string returns

name: python-aws-ai-ml-tools
description: Python Strands @tool wrappers for AWS AI/ML services (Bedrock, Rekognition, Textract, Transcribe, SageMaker) follow simplified pattern with boto3 clients and JSON string returns, mirroring TypeScript implementations

name: python-strands-tool-simple-pattern
description: Python Strands tools use @tool decorator, boto3 clients, and return formatted strings. Simpler than TypeScript tools because they run in single-tenant agent context (no tenantId/agentId params). Pattern: import boto3 + strands.tools.tool decorator + try/except with formatted string responses.

name: parallel-tool-wiring
description: When scaling tool development, dispatch parallel builders for non-overlapping modules (alpha: analytics, beta: compute, gamma: AI/ML, delta: monitoring/DB), then use a wiring agent to merge all branches and perform centralized integration into main agent file. Prevents merge conflicts and ensures atomic registration.

name: python-tool-import-verification
description: After wiring Python tools into agent, verify imports match actual function definitions using grep '^def ' on tool files. Common mismatches occur when TypeScript source tools have different function names than Python translations (e.g., execute_redshift_query vs modify_redshift_cluster).

name: gateway-proxy-factory-pattern
description: create_gateway_proxy_tool() sets __name__/__qualname__/__doc__ on a **kwargs closure BEFORE applying @tool decorator — Strands picks up name/description from these attributes. Lambda client captured in closure at factory time (not call time) via _get_lambda_client() singleton.

content: GatewayToolDiscovery._load() checks AGENTCORE_GATEWAY_ENDPOINT env var: if set, calls _discover_from_gateway() which reads SSM params for Lambda ARNs and creates proxy tools; if not set, falls back to importlib local path. CHIMERA_ENV_NAME controls the SSM prefix (default 'dev').

python-evolution-tools-safety-rails: trigger_infra_evolution enforces 4 safety gates in sequence: (1) SSM kill switch /chimera/evolution/self-modify-enabled/{env} (fail open if missing), (2) Cedar/AVP policy check via CEDAR_POLICY_STORE_ID env var (fail open if unset), (3) DynamoDB daily rate limit per tenant (max 5/day, atomic increment), (4) CDK code validation (non-empty, <100KB, must extend Stack, no AdministratorAccess). All gates fail-open so dev/test works without infra configured.

python-evolution-tools-codecommit-api: trigger_infra_evolution uses CodeCommit CreateCommit API (not subprocess git) to commit CDK stack files to main branch. Requires existing main branch. Creates files at infra/lib/agent-evolved/{capability_name}-stack.ts. _commit_to_codecommit encodes file content as UTF-8 bytes. CodePipeline triggers automatically from CodeCommit push via EventBridge.
